channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Dozens dead after plane goes off runway in South Korea
item.description: Emergency officials said at least 62 passengers are dead after a plane caught on fire after swerving off a runway at an airport in South Korea, the Associated Press reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Plane burst into flames after skidding off runway at an airport in South Korea, killing at least 62
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skid off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy, killing at least 62 people, officials said, in one of the country's worst aviation disa
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump appears to side with Musk, tech allies in debate over foreign workers roiling his supporters
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump appears to be siding with Elon Musk and his other backers in the tech industry as a dispute over immigration visas has divided his supporters.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drought, fires and deforestation battered Amazon rainforest in 2024
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — 2024 was a brutal year for the Amazon rainforest, with rampant wildfires and extreme drought ravaging large parts of a biome that’s a critical counterweight to climate change. A warming climate fed drought that in turn fed the worst year for fires since 2005.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin apologizes over 'tragic' Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash
item.description: Russia’s President Vladimir Putin apologized to Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev over the “tragic” Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash that killed 38 people on board, but stopped short of admitting any responsibility for the incident.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: IDF finds Hezbollah weapons cache in underground tunnel: video
item.description: The Israeli military said it discovered and destroyed an underground tunnel system used by Hezbollah to hide weapons used to attack Israel in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kazakhstan plane crash survivors say they heard bangs before aircraft went down, Putin issues statement
item.description: Survivors of the Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed in Kazakhstan on Christmas Day say they heard at least one loud bang before the aircraft crashed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel detains the director of one of northern Gaza's last functioning hospitals during a raid
item.description: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel’s army has detained the director of one of northern Gaza's last functioning hospitals.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Indian Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, who instituted economic reforms, cremated in New Delhi
item.description: Manmohan Singh, the former Indian prime minister widely regarded as the architect of the country’s economic reform program, has been cremated after a state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Aijaz Hussain, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli airstrikes hit a Yemen airport as a jet with hundreds onboard was landing, UN official says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen’s main airport as a civilian Airbus 320 with hundreds of passengers on board was landing and a U.N. delegation was waiting to leave, the U.N.'s top humanitarian official in Yemen said Friday. Julien Harneis told U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: Russian air defense systems may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner this week, a U.S. official said Friday after an Azerbaijani minister also suggested the plane was hit by a weapon, citing expert analysis and survivor accounts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Azerbaijan Airlines blames deadly plane crash on 'external interference' as Russia speculation grows
item.description: Azerbaijan Airlines has suspended flights to multiple Russian airports in the wake of a deadly plane crash, which it attributed to "physical and technical external interference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about South Korean acting President Han's impeachment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The impeachment of South Korea's acting President Han Duck-soo Friday has plunged the country into further political turmoil, coming less than two weeks after lawmakers impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: President Yoon Suk Yeol off set political crisis in South Korea after imposing martial law. The country's National Assembly has voted to impeach President Han Duck-soo.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Croatia's outspoken president is seeking reelection. He faces several contenders in Sunday's vote
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia’s left-leaning president, an outspoken critic of Western military support for Ukraine in its war against Russia, is running for reelection this weekend against an array of contenders, including the candidate backed by the conservative government.
item.dc:creator: SAŠA KAVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's military intelligence says North Korean troops are suffering heavy battlefield losses
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — North Korean troops are suffering heavy losses in the fighting in Russia's Kursk region and facing logistical difficulties as a result of Ukrainian attacks, Ukraine’s military intelligence said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India's former prime minister Manmohan Singh, architect of economic reforms, dies at 92
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — India’s former Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, widely regarded as the architect of India’s economic reform program and a landmark nuclear deal with the United States, has died. He was 92.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: WHO chief says he was at Yemen airport as Israeli bombs fell nearby
item.description: A wave of Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen's main airport Thursday just as the World Health Organization’s director-general said he was about to board a flight there. One of the U.N. plane’s crew was wounded, WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters that it was "necessary to wait for the end of this probe" before casting any speculation.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden arrives in St. Croix for last vacation as president
item.description: President Biden on Thursday arrived in St. Croix for his last vacation as president before leaving office next month. The president left the White House the day after Christmas with first lady Jill Biden. He arrived in St. Croix in the U.S. Virgin Islands around 2 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy defend Silicon Valley's foreign-born hires
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, co-chairs of President-elect Trump’s new “Department of Government Efficiency,” are defending the tech industry’s reliance on foreign-born engineers as the incoming Trump administration prepares to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Israel launches strikes in Yemen on Houthi military targets, IDF says
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces claimed responsibility for a series of airstrikes in Yemen on Thursday it claimed were targeting Houthi military infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner crashes in Kazakhstan, killing 38 with 29 survivors, officials say
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, killing 38 people and leaving 29 survivors, a Kazakh official said.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia being blamed for Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed hundreds of miles off course, killing dozens
item.description: An Azerbaijan Airlines flight from the Azerbaijani capital of Baku to Russia crashed, hospitalizing survivors while dozens are feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia targets Ukrainian energy infrastructure on Christmas Day
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a massive missile and drone barrage targeting Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Wednesday, striking a thermal power plant and prompting Ukrainians to take shelter in metro stations on Christmas morning.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu wishes a merry Christmas to Christians around the world
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu wished Christians worldwide a Merry Christmas and thanked the Christian community for its support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US military carries out airstrike in Syria, killing 2 ISIS operatives
item.description: The U.S. military conducted an airstrike on Monday in Syria, where they killed a pair of ISIS operatives, wounded another and destroyed a truckload of weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO leaders predict era of 2% defense spending 'probably history' as Trump reportedly floats higher target
item.description: European leaders are making clear ahead of the Trump presidency that they plan to spend more on defense as Russia remains a top threat and as security concerns in the Middle East and North Africa escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation
